title: Define Implementation
text: Define Implementation Define one or more implementations of a component. A component cannot have both Uniface and operating system implementations at the same time. However, a component with a Uniface or operating system implementation can also have one or more of the other non-Uniface implementations. Buttons Properties —define the call-out properties of any implementation type other than Uniface or Java. Call-out is not supported for Java, whereas call-out from one Uniface component to another requires no property settings. For implementations that support both call-in and call-out, such as COM , the call-out mapping is also used as a basis for call-in. New —add implementations to the signature. You cannot add two implementations of the same type. Delete —delete an implementation from the signature. CAUTION: If you delete a Uniface implementation from this list, the corresponding Uniface component is also deleted from the Uniface repository. Fields Type Default Implementation Description Comments —for Uniface component implementations, this field is display-only. How to Define a Component Signature Related Links Component Implementations The component signature specifies the implementation a component, which determines the technology used to deploy it, such as Uniface, C, or Java.
